title: Root Builds on First Profitable Year With Reversal of Q1 Loss to Start 2025
text: Root Inc. reported first-quarter net income of $18.2 million, marking a significant turnaround from a $6.2 million loss in Q1 2024. This result builds on the company's first profitable full year in 2024. Gross premiums written increased 24% year-over-year to approximately $411 million. Combined ratio improved to 95.6, down from 102 in the prior year quarter. CEO Alex Timm noted that the company maintains technological prowess and can quickly react to prevailing trends. The partnership distribution channel grew significantly, now representing 33% of new policy writings. Root added new partnerships with Hyundai Capital America and Experian, featuring embedded insurance at point of sale. The company also launched a product targeting independent agencies. Root operates in 35 states and filed for entry in Michigan during Q1. Previous applications in Massachusetts, New Jersey, and Washington remain pending. CEO Timm stated that tariffs would have a low to mid-single-digit impact on the loss ratio and affirmed the company has sufficient capacity to absorb current cost pressures without immediate rate increases.
